title: Availability, Disaster Recovery, and Validation · Fault Triage
summary: Define recovery objectives and prove branch, service, configuration, and site recovery under realistic failures. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a tested network continuity plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define RPO and RTO from business impact and map them to backup, replication, restoration, and test requirements.
Use MTTR and MTBF as population and process indicators without treating averages as individual guarantees.
Compare cold, warm, and hot sites by readiness, data, network, staffing, cost, and activation time.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Net Dr Testing evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Net Dr Testing assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Run tabletop and validation tests with scenarios, observers, success criteria, rollback, evidence, and remediation owners. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network resilience recovery. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Dr Testing, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a tested network continuity plan while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A written disaster-recovery plan is proven once leadership approves it. This fails the Net Dr Testing contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Zero Trust Architecture (NIST SP 800-207) — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run tabletop and validation tests with scenarios, observers, success criteria, rollback, evidence, and remediation owners.

hints: Name the Net Dr Testing precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network resilience recovery. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A written disaster-recovery plan is proven once leadership approves it.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-dr-testing-1iljhv" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Dr Testing into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-dr-testing-1iljhv
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Net Dr Testing precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network resilience recovery. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A written disaster-recovery plan is proven once leadership approves it.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Dr Testing applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run tabletop and validation tests with scenarios, observers, success criteria, rollback, evidence, and remediation owners.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
